Class Weapon

java.lang.Object
  greenfoot.Actor
      Weapon

public class Weapon
extends greenfoot.Actor

A class that represents a ships weapons. A weapon can move foward, animate as well as check for a collision with an enemy. Weapons will also clean up after themselves so there is no need to manually remove the weapon from the game after a collision of any kind.

Author:
Bretton Murphy

          Weapons damage factor

          Array of greenfootImages for weapons animation

          Weapons travel speed

          Weapons x position in the world

          Weapons y position in the world

Constructor Detail

Weapon

public Weapon(int s,
              int x,
              int y)
Create a weapon with specified position and speed.

Parameters:
s - Speed at which weapon travels.
x - The x position of weapon within the world.
y - The y position of weapon within the world.

Weapon

public Weapon(int s,
              int x,
              int y,
              int slow)
Create a weapon with specified position, speed, slow down factor, and images for animation.

Parameters:
s - Speed at which weapon travels.
x - The x position of weapon within the world.
y - The y position of weapon within the world.
slow - The slow down factor for weapon or how slow the animation will execute.

Weapon

public Weapon(int s,
              int x,
              int y,
              java.lang.String baseName,
              int numOfImages,
              java.lang.String suffix)
Create a weapon with specified position, speed, and images used for animation of the weapon.

Parameters:
s - Speed at which weapon travels.
x - The x position of the weapon within the world.
y - The y position of the weapon within the world.
baseName - The base image name for the weapons animation.
numOfImages - The number of images that are required for the animation.
suffix - The images suffix in the form of ".png"
Method Detail

animate

protected void animate()
Animates wave using images from wave array.

checkEnemyCollision

protected void checkEnemyCollision()
Checks for a collision between this and an enemy or the edge of screen. If so, this will remove itself from the world.

shipFire

protected void shipFire()
Moves weapon forward.
